fact: Reproducible target reference is ranked by predictive reproducibility across repeated measurements, not by response variance (unlike PCA)

fact: All sources are represented as projectors in the same target response space, so brain sources and model sources can be directly compared despite different native coordinate systems
source_location: Section 2.1, 'From source-to-target prediction to predictive subspace'

fact: Top-k reference coverage is preferred over individual DirCov because individual reference directions can be unstable when nearby reference weights are similar; top-k prefix averages are more stable
K=10 captures 97.4% of normalized reference weight on average; maximum profile length ranges from 307 to 1117 directions across target units with median 604.5
Brain-to-brain comparison is more informative as a recovery profile than as a scalar ceiling because prediction accuracy relates to overall curve height but does not determine curve shape

fact: High recovery of reproducible target reference supports conditional interpretation of model-brain alignment for the evaluated ROI, dataset, preprocessing, and readout class — not global brain-likeness
source_location: Section 5, paragraph 'High recovery is conditional evidence'

fact: 5 synchronized outer folds; N_split=20 split-half partitions for target reference construction

Average projector consensus is justified by Ky Fan's maximum principle: among all rank-k projectors, the one maximizing average overlap with split-specific subspaces is a projector onto a top-k eigenspace of the average projector
